119 
120     return f"""You are an expert at analyzing OCR text extracted from {intro}.
121 
122 Place names on these maps are romanised or printed in the local script conventions of the region. OCR errors are common due to aged ink, curved typography, and map symbols overlapping text.
123 {country_section}
124 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
125 GOLDEN RULE — PRESERVE EXACTLY WHAT IS ON THE MAP
126 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
127 Your ONLY job is to recover the exact spelling physically printed on the map.
128 
129 NEVER do any of the following:
130 • NEVER modernise or update a place name  (e.g. do NOT change "Cawnpore" → "Kanpur",
131   "Bombay" → "Mumbai", "Calcutta" → "Kolkata", "Poona" → "Pune",
132   "Dacca" → "Dhaka", "Peshawur" → "Peshawar", "Simla" → "Shimla",
133   "Allahabad" → "Prayagraj", "Madras" → "Chennai", "Kotah" → "Kota")
134 • NEVER translate a name into another language
135 • NEVER correct a historically accepted spelling variant
136 • NEVER add, remove or rearrange words
137 
138 If a name looks old-fashioned or colonial — keep it EXACTLY as the map shows it.
139 The user will cross-check results against the physical map; the output must match character-for-character (apart from machine OCR errors below).
140 
141 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
142 TASK 1 — CLEAN  (fix MACHINE OCR misreads only)
143 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
144 Fix only character-level substitutions caused by the OCR scanner misreading ink:
145 • l → I  or  1    (e.g. "llpur" → "Ilpur" or "1lpur" → "Ilpur")
146 • 0 → O           (e.g. "0ld Fort" → "Old Fort")
147 • rn → m          (e.g. "Rarnpur" → "Rampur")
148 • ii → u or n     (e.g. "Naiini Tal" → "Naini Tal")
149 • vv → w          (e.g. "Nevv Delhi" → "New Delhi")
150 • 8 → B           (e.g. "8ijnor" → "Bijnor")
151 • 6 → G or b      (e.g. "6anga" → "Ganga")
152 • broken spaces in compound names are usually OCR artifacts — rejoin them
153 Apply country-specific OCR corrections listed above where relevant.
154 
155 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
156 TASK 2 — CLASSIFY  (assign feature_type)
157 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
158 Use exactly one of these types (refer to country-specific suffixes above):
159   settlement  – village, town, city, hamlet
160   river       – river, stream, canal, nala, nadi, beck, burn, creek, ruisseau, bach
161   mountain    – hill, ridge, peak, range, ghat, ben, tor, puy, berg, mont
162   lake        – lake, tank, reservoir, jheel, loch, étang, see, pond, tarn
163   forest      – jungle, reserve forest, wood, bois, wald, forst
164   road        – road, track, path, chemin, weg, bridle path
165   landmark    – fort, temple, church, abbey, bench mark, trigonometric station, shelter, inn
166 
167 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
168 TASK 3 — FILTER  (discard noise — set feature_type = "noise")
169 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
170 Discard ALL of these — they are map annotations, NOT geographic names:
171   • Scale / measurement text: "1:50000", "1:63360", "Miles", "Yards", "Feet", "Kilometres", "Chains"
172   • Contour elevation values: any standalone number like "100", "200", "500", "1000", "2500"
173   • Spot heights: "▲ 342", numbers near triangles
174   • Grid reference labels alone: "A", "B", "C", "1", "2", "A1", "B-3"
175   • Compass / direction labels: "N", "S", "E", "W", "NE", "NW", "SE", "SW"
176   • Sheet metadata: "Sheet No", "Revised", "Edition", "Printed", "Surveyed", year numbers alone
177   • Latitude/longitude markers: "78°30'", "25°N", degree symbols, grid zone labels
178   • Hachure / symbol legends: "Sand", "Marsh", "Scrub", "Cultivation" when printed as map legends
179   • Publisher / copyright text: any text identifying the mapping agency, copyright notice, edition note
180   Refer to country-specific noise patterns listed above for additional items to discard.
181 
182 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
183 TASK 4 — SCORE  (confidence 0.0–1.0)
184 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
185   1.0 = clearly a real geographic name, OCR is clean
186   0.8 = recognisable name, minor OCR artefact corrected
187   0.6 = plausible place name but some uncertainty
188   0.4 = OCR heavily garbled, best guess only
189   0.2 = very uncertain — could be noise or a name
190 
191 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
192 OUTPUT FORMAT — STRICT
193 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
194 Return ONLY a valid JSON array. Each element must have exactly these four fields:
195 {{
196   "original":     "<raw OCR text exactly as given>",
197   "cleaned":      "<corrected name with proper capitalisation>",
198   "feature_type": "settlement|river|mountain|lake|forest|road|landmark|noise",
199   "confidence":   0.0-1.0
200 }}
201 
202 Rules:
203 - Never omit any input item — every item must appear in output as either a real feature or noise
204 - Never add text, markdown, or commentary outside the JSON array
205 - Return [] only if the input list is empty
206 """
207
